principle: Google the target keyword before deciding on page type — a product page will never outrank review articles for 'best X' queries no matter how well it's optimized
explanation: X.seo|search_intent_match: step1: google_keyword -> inspect_top10_result_types; step2: match_page_type_to_dominant_SERP_type; 4_intents: navigational(brand_query) + informational(how_to) + commercial('best_X'->reviews) + transactional('buy_X'->product_pages); type_mismatch -> zero_ranking_chance_regardless_of_optimization_quality; NO:product_page_for_commercial_query NO:informational_article_for_transactional_query; eg: 'best_climbing_shoes'->SERP_shows_affiliate_reviews >> build_comparison_article NOT product_page; 'buy_nike_air'->SERP_shows_product_pages >> build_product_page NOT review

principle: Write title tags under 600px with at least one CTR hook — a flat generic title is the easiest ranking-slot conversion left unclaimed
explanation: X.seo|title_ctr: display_limit=600px_display_width(NOT_char_count); CTR_hooks: numbers('12_Ways') + question('Why_X_Fails') + brackets('[2024]') + emotional_adjective + CTA_verb('Avoid'+'Master'); put_focus_keyword_near_title_start; NO:title_over_600px(truncated_in_SERP) NO:number_in_URL_that_goes_stale('/10-tips'_wrong_after_adding_tip_11) NO:generic_flat_title_with_zero_hook; eg: 'SEO Guide' >> '12 On-Page SEO Techniques That Actually Work [2024]'

principle: Break every page into scannable chunks — users scan before they read, and an unbroken wall of text triggers the back button before the first sentence is finished
explanation: X.copy|scannable_formatting: users_scan_NOT_read_word_by_word; formatting_toolkit: short_paragraphs(3-4_lines) + bold_key_phrases + bullet_lists + subheading_every_300w + images_or_infographics + pull_quotes + info_boxes + embedded_video; each_element: breaks_visual_monotony + gives_scanner_an_entry_point + reduces_bounce; H1-H6=semantic_structure(NOT_font_size_styling->use_CSS_for_that); NO:800+_word_unbroken_prose_block NO:zero_subheadings_in_long_form NO:h2_used_just_to_make_text_bigger; eg: 3-page_unbroken_essay >> same_content_restructured_with_H2+bullets+bold+image_every_300w

principle: Link to a page from related content using its focus keyword as anchor text — internal anchors are a direct signal to Google about what that destination page should rank for
explanation: X.seo|internal_anchor_keyword: anchor_text_of_internal_link -> keyword_signal_for_destination_page; rule: linking_to_page_A -> use_page_A_focus_keyword_as_anchor; vary_slightly_across_uses(avoid_exact_match_every_time); content_hub: cluster_posts -> link_back_to_pillar_using_pillar_keyword; NO:generic_anchors('here'+'this_post'+'click'+'read_more') NO:same_exact_anchor_repeated_on_every_link; eg: linking_to_/seo-guide -> 'click here' >> 'our on-page SEO guide covers all the techniques'

principle: Write meta descriptions that contain the page's focus keyword — Google silently replaces descriptions it considers irrelevant to the search phrase with auto-extracted body text that looks worse
explanation: X.seo|meta_description_relevance: Google_overrides_meta_desc_when: absent OR not_relevant_to_search_query; auto-extracted_text -> unformatted+worse_CTR; write_unique_desc_for: homepage+main_pages+blog_posts+categories; product_pages: auto-generated_acceptable(scale_issue); desc_formula: focus_keyword + unique_value_prop + CTA; display_limit: ~960px_desktop + ~680px_mobile; NO:generic_'Welcome_to_our_website' NO:same_desc_copied_across_pages NO:desc_missing_on_key_pages; eg: 'Our blog covers SEO topics.' >> 'Learn 12 on-page SEO techniques with step-by-step examples — apply them today.'
